message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): mid-flight cx-cancel on run_web_server_with_cx (tick 417)

Pins the orchestrator-level cx→shutdown wiring for
`run_web_server_with_cx`. Previously only the pre-start timing was
covered (tick 323's `web_server_with_cx_pre_cancelled_refuses_to_bind`);
the mid-flight path through `wait_for_shutdown_signal_with_cx`'s
cancel_fut branch — poll-sleep loop on `cx.is_cancel_requested()` every
100 ms via `sleep_with_cx`, then graceful `signal_shutdown` +
`poke_listener` + drain — had no direct test coverage.

The new `web_server_with_cx_mid_flight_cancel_exits_cleanly` test:
- Spawns `run_web_server_with_cx` in a task (port 0 ephemeral bind)
- Lets it bind + enter its `select! { join | shutdown_signal }` loop
- Cancels cx 200 ms later via a separate task
- Asserts the orchestrator future resolves to `Ok(())` within 5 s

Locally observed: full server lifecycle + graceful shutdown completes
in under 0.3 s on a warm test run.

Complements tick 313 (pre-cancel HTTP client) and tick 322 (metrics
server mid-flight cancel) to pin all three cx-signal timings (pre,
mid, happy) across the three bead-scoped service boundaries (HTTP
client, metrics server, web server). Filter `web_server_with_cx_`
picks up both this test and the tick-323 pre-cancel test, so check
script run 5/6 count rises from 1 to 2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
